authors: Deer TR, Levy RM, Kramer J, Poree L, Amirdelfan K, Grigsby E, Staats P, Burton AW, Burgher AH, Obray J, et al.
title: Dorsal root ganglion stimulation yielded higher treatment success rate for complex regional pain syndrome and causalgia at 3 and 12 months
study_question: Does DRG stimulation outperform SCS for lower-limb CRPS or causalgia pain outcomes?
population_assessed: 152 adults with lower-extremity complex regional pain syndrome or causalgia, randomized to DRG or SCS.
outcome_measures: Treatment success, pain reduction, paresthesia variation, quality of life, mood, adverse events.
inclusion_criteria: Lower-limb CRPS or causalgia, pain at least 6 months, VAS above 60, failed two drugs.
answer_to_study_question: DRG showed higher treatment success and pain relief, with less postural paresthesia variation, than SCS.
reasoning: All models strongly agreed on citation details, design, year, DOI, PubMed link, follow-up, and sample size. I used the shared title without subtitle because one model omitted it. Authors were truncated with et al. to satisfy length limits. Clinical findings merged consistent outcome details across models.
